title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Contract Lens: Promise Callback Api
summary: A service mixes callback and promise APIs, assumes publish returns a Promise, trusts TypeScript types as runtime validation, and exits before asynchronous close and confirms settle. Planned artifact: Add and verify the promise callback api increment in typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.
Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Promise Callback Api · Theory Promise Callback Api
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8608 in a canary consumer reading old and new envelope versions. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Promise Callback Api contract for the supplied data.
